mappings.rationaleAI: Urban planning covering metropolitan growth, transportation infrastructure, and resilience to disasters addresses basic engineering services (A), urban transportation systems (A.3), and design of recreational facilities as social services (B.2).
Civil engineering covering bridges, tunnels, water management, and urbanization addresses basic engineering services (A), water-supply systems (A.1), and sanitation systems (A.2).
Global suburban development covering automobile-dependent housing, mass transit, and employment patterns addresses urban transportation systems (A.3) and interurban transportation (A.4).
Economic analysis of pollution control and environmental damage reduction directly addresses control of air, water, and land pollution (C.1).
Environmental politics covering climate change policy, pollution regulation, and tensions between protection and growth addresses control of pollution (C.1).
Environmental law covering regulatory frameworks for pollution control and environmental standards directly addresses control of air, water, land, and other pollution (C.1).
Public health covering sanitation, disease prevention, and population health management connects to sanitation systems (A.2).
Criminology covering crime measurement, policing, and criminal justice policy addresses police technology (B.1) and crime control technology (B.1.b).
Forensic science covering DNA profiling, fingerprints, and crime scene investigation directly addresses crime control technology (B.1.b).
Physical and chemical properties of water and its significance for Earth's systems connect to urban water-supply systems (A.1).
Design of parks, gardens, and urban environments directly addresses design, construction, and maintenance of recreational facilities (B.2).
Sustainability addressing human impacts on planetary systems and sustainable production patterns connects to technological responses to urban problems (C) and pollution control (C.1).
Climate change covering global warming, decarbonization solutions, and restructuring industrial economies connects to control of air pollution (C.1).
Energy conversion and distribution for electricity generation connects to the technology of electric power in urban settings (A.5).
